scope: 3D_test only. Visual strategy taxonomy and screening-effect metadata for testing before main pipeline integration.
effect_metadata_note: Temperature effects are screening ranges for early option comparison, not dynamic simulation outputs. Perceived cooling is separated from actual operative-temperature reduction where relevant.
placement_principles: Every generated object must be hosted to a detected wall, opening, floor, ceiling, or validated support plane.
Window-covering rails mount close to the interior wall face and above the detected opening head.
Sliding or balcony doors must preserve a clear active passage; side-mounted greenery is preferred over front-mounted obstruction.
Old-building apartment retrofits avoid roof-dependent interventions, structural exterior changes without permission, and heavy wet assemblies.
Biophilic elements are comfort/restorative visual layers unless separately validated as shading or ventilation interventions.
Numerical effects should be displayed as confidence-bounded screening values, never as guaranteed performance.

strategies.label: Interior window covering layer
strategies.mounting: interior wall/opening head, 0.08-0.16 m from wall face

academic_basis: ASHRAE Fundamentals Handbook internal shading / SHGC reduction logic
screening_effects.project_warning: Internal fabric shading is usually weaker than exterior shading because solar radiation has already entered the room.

strategies.label: Solar-control glazing tint
strategies.mounting: changes existing glazing material/colour; no added second pane in the visual test

academic_basis: CTE DB-HE 2022 envelope and solar-control logic
Perez-Lombard et al. (2008), Energy and Buildings

strategies.label: External shading layer
strategies.mounting: exterior facade/balcony only after permission and safety checks

academic_basis: Henze et al. (2004), Energy and Buildings
Perez et al. green facade review for planter shading
screening_effects.project_warning: Exterior shading should be prioritized over internal coverings where permission and safety allow.

strategies.label: Ceiling fan air movement
strategies.mounting: ceiling-hosted fan centered in occupied zone, clear of curtains and tall furniture

screening_effects.project_warning: A fan improves felt comfort and heat-stress tolerance but does not fix solar gain or envelope heat transfer.

strategies.label: Wall insulation reinforcement layer
strategies.mounting: thin inward layer over existing wall; does not extend facade outward

screening_effects.project_warning: Interior insulation must be checked for moisture, condensation, and thermal bridge risk before specification.

strategies.label: Floor plant cluster
strategies.mounting: floor-based, beside opening or corner, never in door sweep/path

academic_basis: Urban green infrastructure and heat-health adaptation literature
Biophilic design literature; not a direct room thermal compliance source
screening_effects.project_warning: Indoor planting should not drive benchmark pass/fail unless it is part of a validated shading strategy.

strategies.label: Wall-adjacent planting layer
strategies.mounting: use sideHost wall strip beside detected opening; close to wall face, side-shifted away from active glazing and door path

conditional_upgrade.condition: if exterior-facing vegetation directly shades the opening or facade

academic_basis: Perez et al. (2011), Energy and Buildings for green facade shading
Biophilic design literature for restorative/wellbeing support
screening_effects.project_warning: Interior trellis/plant shelves are mainly visual-comfort measures unless their geometry shades solar-exposed glazing.

strategies.label: Hanging lightweight greenery
strategies.mounting: wall/opening-head rail on sideHost wall strip, with visible bracket placeholders and drip-safe hanging pot logic

academic_basis: Biophilic design literature; not a direct room thermal compliance source
screening_effects.project_warning: Use as a support layer, not the primary thermal retrofit.

support_placeholder_system.purpose: Temporary procedural supports explain how future GLB components attach to the room before realistic assets are injected.
rules: Rails and brackets are shown when an item needs a wall/opening-head support.
Shelves are represented as wall-hosted console shelves with visible brackets.
Plant ladders are wall-adjacent freestanding supports, not objects floating over glazing.
Trellis screens are wall-mounted panels beside the opening, with foliage biased outward into the room.
Objects with uncertain fixing remain conditional until user or technical confirmation.
Numerical-effect ranges are shown beside assets as screening metadata and must be validated before final reporting.

local_axes.z: offset inward from the wall face toward the room
